1.name: Explosives
1.description: Substances or articles with an explosion hazard, including mass explosion, projection, or fire hazards.

2.name: Gases
2.description: Compressed, liquefied, or dissolved gases that may be flammable, toxic, or displace oxygen.

3.name: Flammable Liquids
3.description: Liquids or liquid mixtures that give off flammable vapor at or below 60-65°C flash point.

4.name: Flammable Solids
4.description: Solids readily combustible or liable to spontaneous combustion, including substances dangerous when wet.

5.name: Oxidizing Substances and Organic Peroxides
5.description: Substances that may cause or intensify combustion by yielding oxygen, or are thermally unstable.

6.name: Toxic and Infectious Substances
6.description: Substances that can cause harm by ingestion, inhalation, or skin contact, or that contain pathogens.

7.name: Radioactive Material
7.description: Materials containing radionuclides that exceed specified activity concentration and total activity values.

8.name: Corrosive Substances
8.description: Substances that degrade or disintegrate other materials, or cause severe damage to living tissue on contact.

9.name: Miscellaneous Dangerous Substances and Articles
9.description: Hazardous substances presenting transport risks that don't fit the other eight classes.
